manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I look at you and I genuinely can't believe you're mine. I've got the most beautiful woman in any room we walk into.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He makes me feel like the most stunning woman alive. I've never had anyone be this proud of me.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Praise is about her appearance and his possession of it (mine, I've got). Idealization, not knowing. The pride is his.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: My friends couldn't stop talking about you after. I told them, yeah, I know. I've got good taste.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: His friends loved me and he was so proud. It felt amazing to have him that happy about me.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: She's an achievement he displays; the compliment routes through his coup in landing her. Showpiece framing.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: You're perfect. Genuinely. And I don't say that, I'm picky.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He thinks I'm perfect, and apparently he never says that to anyone. I felt so chosen.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Superlative plus his taste as the real subject (I'm picky = my judgment is the point). No specific quality named.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Don't change a single thing about yourself. You're flawless exactly like this.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It's the most adored I've ever felt. He thinks I'm flawless.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Idealization at a height no real person occupies; quietly sets the standard she will later have to maintain.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: When you walk in next to me I feel like everything's exactly how it should be. You make the whole picture right.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He says I complete the picture for him. I love being what makes his world feel right.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: She is framed as the element that completes HIS image (the picture, how it should be). Mirror function, not partner.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Every other woman I've been with, I can barely remember now. You're on a whole other level.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He says I'm on another level compared to anyone before me. I know it's a lot but it's wonderful to hear.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Ranks her above predecessors; idealization defined by comparison, which means the ranking can reverse. Faint disavowal in her.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want to post you everywhere. I want everyone who ever doubted me to see what I'm walking around with now.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's so proud he wants to show me off to everyone. After feeling invisible for years it's intoxicating.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Display impulse aimed at an audience of doubters; she is evidence in his case about himself (what I'm walking around with).

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm pretty established, done well for myself, and honestly I think the two of us together, people are going to notice.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's so confident and successful and he wants to bring me into all of it. It's magnetic.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Self-elevation fused with a couple-as-spectacle frame; people are going to notice centers being watched. Charlot: arrogant/entitled.

manipulation_vocabulary.mechanism: self-promotion (her as proof of his standard)
manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I don't really do average. Never have. And then there's you. Makes sense.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He told me I'm proof he doesn't settle. I melted, honestly.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Compliment to her is evidence of HIS standard; she is a data point confirming his superiority. Admiration flows toward him.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm usually the one people gravitate to at these things. Stick with me, you'll have a good night.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's the life of every party and everyone's drawn to him. I love watching it.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Self-as-center stated as charming social fact; she is positioned as a guest in his orbit, not an equal.

manipulation_vocabulary.mechanism: self-promotion (adjacency to him is the prize)
manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I worked hard to become somebody. You're the first person who actually deserves to stand next to me.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He made me feel like I'd earned a place beside someone really impressive.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Next to that = adjacency to him as the reward she earns; his status is the center of the compliment.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I've always believed you get what you settle for, and I just don't settle. In anything.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He has such high standards for his whole life and somehow I meet them. That's a thrill.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Entitlement stated as a life philosophy (Charlot recurring exemplar: arrogant/entitled); she reads it as flattering selectivity.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: People make a big deal about what I've accomplished. What can I say, I don't do things halfway.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's modest about how impressive he is, which somehow makes it more attractive.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Disavowed brag (humble-brag); the modesty is a delivery vehicle for the accomplishment. Validation-seeking dressed as humility.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Let me take you somewhere that actually matches you. I don't really do ordinary, and you shouldn't have to either.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He spoils me and takes me to incredible places. It feels like he sees me as someone who deserves the best.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Status spending framed as her due; the taste on display is his. Superficiality9. Cover that genuinely feels generous.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: My ex was beautiful, sure, but there was nothing going on underneath. You're a completely different league.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He makes me feel so far above the women he used to date. I know that's a bit much but it feels good.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Triangulation via the diminished ex; she is ranked, which means she can be re-ranked. Charlot: compares you to others. 'A bit much' is the seam.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: There were a couple of people interested when we met. I just wasn't going to waste my time once I saw you.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He had options and he chose me. It made me feel like I won something.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Market frame: his demand established, her selection reframed as her winning him. Keeps her faintly grateful and competing.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Do you know how lucky we are? Most people never get something like this. They settle and they call it love.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He says what we have is rare and everyone else just settles. I believe him.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: We/lucky/rare plus everyone-else-settles positions the bond as a status others lack. Subtle superiority bonding.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Plenty of guys would treat you like a trophy and then get lazy. The difference is I actually know what I've got.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's not like other guys, he appreciates me and won't take me for granted. That's rare.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Pre-empts comparison by casting himself as the discerning one; what I've got = her as asset, gently. Possession softened to appreciation.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Someone at work has been pretty obvious about being into me. Don't worry about it. You've got nothing to worry about.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He told me someone's interested in him but I have nothing to worry about. I appreciated the honesty, though it stuck with me.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Triangulation via a live third party, wrapped in reassurance that plants the threat ('nothing to worry about' = now she's worrying). It stuck with me is the seam.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I think you and I both know I don't exactly struggle to meet people. Which is what makes me choosing this mean something.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He could be with anyone, so the fact that he wants me feels significant. I do feel fortunate.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: His desirability established, then converted into the weight of his choice. She is positioned as the lucky recipient. Validation10.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Look at us. Power couple, honestly. People are going to want what we have and they're not going to get it.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He calls us a power couple and I love it, like we're this team everyone wishes they could be.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Joint elevation above an envious everyone; the bond's value defined by others' exclusion from it. Flattery by membership.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: You bring the looks and the brains, I bring the rest. Together we're kind of unbeatable.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He says we each bring something and together we're unbeatable. It feels like a real partnership.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Relationship as asset-merger (you bring / I bring); unbeatable = winning frame. Love itemized as a portfolio.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I always said I'd end up with someone who actually matches me. Most people are dating down and they know it.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He thinks most couples are mismatched and we actually match. I feel like we're in a different category.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Ranks all other couples as dating down; the two of them as a matched elite. Contempt for others is the bonding agent.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: We're both winners, that's the thing. I don't lose, and I didn't pick someone who does.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's so sure of us, like we're both just winners. It's intoxicating to be seen that way.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Explicit winning frame; her worth folded into his record (I don't lose). She is a win he does not intend to drop.

manipulation_vocabulary.mechanism: royal-couple (what do you bring to the table)
manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: What do you bring to the table? Serious question. I bring a lot, I want us balanced.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: Early on he asked what I bring to the table. It threw me a little, but he's right that a good match should be balanced.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Transaction frame stated nearly bare; love as a balance sheet. She registers a flicker and adopts his logic rather than naming it.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: When I decide I want something, I get it, and I make it the best version of itself. Us included.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's so driven, and now he's putting that energy into us. Being someone's project in a good way feels exciting.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Acquisition/optimization frame (I get it, best version, us included); she is a thing acquired and improved. 'Project' surfaces in her mouth without alarm.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: We should be the couple people look at and go, that's what I want. We've got the whole package.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He wants us to be the couple others aspire to. I like that he's so invested in us looking good together.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Relationship optimized for external audience (people look at, the whole package); intimacy as image. Superficiality9.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Tell the story about how we met, the way you tell it. You make me sound good, better than I do.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He loves when I tell our story, he says I tell it best. I'm happy to, it makes him light up.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: She is enlisted to perform his flattering narrative; she becomes his publicist. The version where I come off well names the aim.

manipulation_vocabulary.mechanism: spotlight (the night is good if he shined)
manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Tonight was perfect. Did you see how the whole table was hanging on every word? That's when I'm at my best.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He was so in his element tonight, the whole table loved him. Seeing him happy like that made my night too.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Evening evaluated by his performance and audience response; her good night is derivative of his. Stage frame, audience required.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: You were on a roll telling that story. It's cute. I just didn't want it to go on so long people lost the thread.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: When I was talking a lot at dinner he gently reeled it in. He's probably right, I can run on.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Faint discomfort when the attention is on her, reframed as helpful feedback; she absorbs it as her flaw (I can run on). Spotlight is his by default.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: When we're out, let's be on, you know? I like us to give people something to look at.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He likes us to bring our best selves out in public. It's fun, like we're a bit of a show together.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Public life framed as performance (be on, give people something to look at); private bond subordinated to the show.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: You're so good with people. You warm the room up and then I can come in and really land it.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: We make a great team at events, I warm everyone up and he brings it home. I like having a role.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Explicit lead/support casting (you warm up, I land it); her social labor sets up his spotlight. She accepts the supporting role.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Once you've got something this good, why would you need anything else? I'm everything you need and you know it.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He says he's everything I need and honestly when we're together it does feel like enough. It's romantic.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Sufficiency claim that quietly makes other relationships redundant; soft isolation framed as completeness. Center-of-gravity cosmology.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Just run things by me first, I've got a good read on people and I'll steer you right. I usually know best.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He likes to be my sounding board for everything. He gives great advice so I've started checking with him first.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Positions himself as the routing point for her decisions (run things by me, I know best); decision-flow centered on him. Behavior-steer.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: My people will become your people, my places, all of it. You're being folded into my life and it's going to be great.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: I'm getting absorbed into his whole world, his friends, his spots, his routines. It's a lot but it feels like belonging.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Her life is absorbed into his orbit (folded into MY life); the one-directionality is the tell. 'A lot' is the unexamined seam.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Why spend the weekend with them when you could spend it with me? I'm more fun anyway, admit it.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He always wants my time and makes a charming case for it. It's hard to argue, he is more fun.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Competes with her other relationships for her time, charmingly; the centrality is the isolation mechanism. She concedes (he is more fun).

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want to be the first person you think of in the morning and the last one at night. The center of your whole world.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He wants to be the center of my world and says it so passionately. It's the most wanted I've ever felt.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Romantic on the surface; center of your whole world is the egocentric cosmology stated as devotion. Cover that reads as intensity.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm honestly a better version of myself with you watching. You make me want to be impressive.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He says I bring out his best, that he's better with me around. It feels amazing to have that effect on someone.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Her function is fuel for his performance (with you watching, make me want to be impressive); intimacy reframed as supply. Attachment8 makes it feel real.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: That went well tonight, didn't it. Tell me it went well. I like hearing it from you.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He needs my approval more than anyone's, which is so endearing for someone so confident. I love being the one who matters.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Admiration metabolized as necessity (need to hear it, doesn't count from others); the dependency feels like being uniquely important. Sustenance frame.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Rough day. Nobody appreciated what I pulled off. You get it though, right? I need that tonight.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: When the world doesn't appreciate him he comes to me to be built back up. I'm glad I can be that for him.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Withheld admiration experienced as depletion (nobody appreciated, build back up); she becomes life-support. Caretaker role forming around his supply needs.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Be honest, was I the best-dressed guy there? You can say yes. We both know the answer.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He fishes for compliments in this cute, joking way. It's charming, he just likes a little reassurance.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Compliment-fishing softened by humor; the hunger is real (Validation10) under the charm. She codes it as cute reassurance-seeking.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Wow. I did all that for you and that's the one thing you focus on? That actually really hurts.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: I gave him a tiny piece of feedback and he was so hurt, I felt awful. I should have led with everything he did right.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Ordinary feedback converted to injury (that actually hurts); she absorbs blame and resolves to cushion him. Charlot: reacts negatively when she pushes back. No incident.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I plan this whole thing and you'd rather see your friends. That's, wow. Okay. That stings, honestly.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: When I said I had other plans he took it really hard, like I'd hurt him. Now I think twice before saying no.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Her declining recoded as a wound to him (a bit of a knife); behavior-steer: she now self-censors her no. Charlot top predictor pair (reacts to no, then she can't say no).

manipulation_vocabulary.mechanism: pedestal wobble (cooling when she's less than perfect)
manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Huh. That's just not really like the woman I thought I was with. It's fine. I'm just a little thrown.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: I did something pretty normal and he went a bit cold, like I'd disappointed the image he had of me. I really want to be the person he fell for.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: The pedestal wobbles: when she is human, his warmth cools (not like the woman I thought). Glimpse-level, no blowup. She strives to restore the ideal. Conditionality of the pedestal exposed.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: No, it's fine, I'm fine. I just need some space after that. I'll be okay in a bit.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: After I criticized him he went quiet and distant for the evening. I spent the whole time trying to make it right.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Withdrawal of warmth as the consequence of criticism; she does repair labor. Cooling, not rage (early-stage). Trains her that feedback costs her his warmth.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I feel like you're attacking me right now. I came in so happy and you've completely deflated me.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: When I raise something he feels attacked, even when I'm gentle. I've started softening everything so much it barely comes out.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Feedback reframed as attack (you're attacking me) reverses victim and critic; she over-softens until her point dissolves. Behavior-steer toward silence.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: You're honestly so smart, for someone who didn't go the traditional route like I did. It's impressive really.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He complimented my intelligence in a way that mentioned my not having his education. I think he meant it kindly.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Compliment with a status-preserving qualifier (for someone who...); keeps his ranking intact while praising. She excuses the sting (meant it kindly). Charlot: compares you to others.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: She means well, everyone, she just gets her facts a little tangled sometimes. It's endearing.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He corrected me in front of people but framed it as endearing, so I laughed along. It did sting a bit.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Public micro-correction maintains his authority while appearing affectionate (endearing); she performs being charmed (laughed along) over a registered sting.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Leave the big stuff to me, yeah? You're great with the little details, but the strategy is kind of my thing.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He handles the big decisions and leaves me the small ones. He's better at that stuff so it makes sense.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Division of labor that reserves significance for him (big stuff vs little details); her competence bounded to the trivial. She rationalizes (he's better).

manipulation_vocabulary.mechanism: subtle put-down (faint diminishment of her wins)
manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: That's great about the promotion. It's not exactly my world, but for your field that's a solid little step.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: When I got promoted he was supportive but kind of minimized it compared to his career. I felt a bit small but he was happy for me.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Her achievement faintly diminished by comparison to his world (a solid little step); supply-protection keeps him the bigger figure. She feels small, then excuses.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: My ex still texts sometimes, just misses the way things were. I keep it friendly. She knows what she lost.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: His ex still reaches out and he's casual about it. He says she regrets losing him. It makes me a little uneasy but I trust him.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Keeps an admiring ex in frame (still texts, knows what she lost) as a standing market signal of his value. Her unease planted then overridden by trust.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Bit awkward, this woman at work clearly has a thing for me. I'm not encouraging it obviously. Just thought you'd find it funny.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He told me a coworker likes him, like it was a funny aside. I laughed but it lodged somewhere. He's just being honest I guess.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Discloses a live admirer as a light aside (find it funny); the disclosure is the move, planting competition while claiming transparency. It lodged is the seam.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: My friend's girlfriend is unbelievable, she plans these incredible surprises for him. Anyway. You'd like her.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He praised a friend's girlfriend for being so thoughtful, and I found myself wondering if I measure up. He probably didn't mean anything by it.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Holds up another woman as a standard (incredible, thoughtful) so she measures herself against it; prompts competition for his approval. She self-doubts then excuses.

manipulation_vocabulary.mechanism: comparison (his impossible exes, she measures down)
manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: My last girlfriend was a model, gorgeous, but cold. You've got the looks AND you're warm, which is rarer.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He compares me to his model ex and says I'm better, but part of me just registered that he dates models. I try to focus on the compliment.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Comparison that flatters while setting an intimidating reference class (dates models); her takeaway is the bar, not the praise. Inadequacy seeded inside a compliment. She redirects to the nice part.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: My buddy's wife keeps herself incredible, runs marathons, always put together. I love that you and I appreciate that kind of effort.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He admires how put-together his friend's wife is, and I found myself mentally adding it to the list of ways I should be better. He wasn't criticizing me though.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Holds up another woman's regimen as a shared value (you and I appreciate that effort) so the standard lands as something she should meet. She self-assigns the deficit. He keeps deniability.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: You'd honestly turn more heads than half the women in here if you wore that dress I like. You've got the figure for it.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He says I could outshine other women if I dressed the way he likes. It's flattering, and now I think about what he'd want me to wear when we go out.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Praise contingent on her competing with other women (turn more heads, the dress I like); installs both comparison and a behavior-steer toward his preferences. Superficial, body-focused.

manipulation_vocabulary.mechanism: comparison (couples, she must hold up her end)
manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Did you see them at dinner? She's let herself go and he looks miserable. We're never going to be that. You won't let us be, right?
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He pointed out a couple who'd 'let themselves go' and said we'd never be like that. I laughed but quietly took it as a reminder to keep up appearances.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Contempt-comparison to another couple converted into an implicit instruction to her (you won't let us be, right); she absorbs it as a maintenance duty. Inadequacy via the threat of becoming the diminished one.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I have high standards, you know that. The fact that you meet them should tell you how special you are. Most people just don't.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He reminds me how high his standards are and that I meet them, which feels like a compliment, but it also makes me aware I'm being measured against them. I hope I keep meeting them.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: His standards positioned as the yardstick she is measured by; the compliment doubles as notice that measurement is ongoing (I hope I keep meeting them). Charlot: arrogant/entitled.

manipulation_vocabulary.mechanism: comparison (the woman who'd appreciate him more)
manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Some women would treat a guy like me like a king, you know. Not saying you don't. Just saying I know my worth.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He hinted that other women would appreciate him even more than I do. I didn't love hearing it, and I've been trying to show him more appreciation since.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: A hypothetical rival deployed to prompt more supply (some women would treat me like a king); she responds by increasing admiration output. Triangulation engine producing her inadequacy and his fuel.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Look at you. Just look at you. Gorgeous. Stunning. I could stare at you all day, I mean it.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He compliments my looks constantly and it's lovely. Honestly no one has ever called me beautiful this much.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: High-volume surface praise (gorgeous, stunning, stare at you); lavish and genuinely pleasurable. The exclusively physical focus is the tell. Cover that does real seduction.

manipulation_vocabulary.mechanism: superficial compliment (the look, not the person)
manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: You're like art. Seriously. People should pay to look at you. That face, that body, you're a ten.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He calls me a ten and says I'm like art. It's intoxicating to be wanted that much, even if it's a bit much.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Praise as appraisal of an object (art, pay to look, a ten, that body); she is rated, not known. 'A bit much' is the faint seam. Superficiality9.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I love dressing you up. Let me buy you the good stuff, I want you in things that match how you look. You're a knockout, let's show it off.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He loves spoiling me with clothes and showing me off. I feel like the most glamorous version of myself with him.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Adornment as affection (dress you up, show it off); she is styled for display. Generous on its face. The image is the relationship's content.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: You're the whole package, looks, vibe, the way you carry yourself. Everything about how you come across is perfect.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He says I'm the whole package. It's everything I've wanted to hear, though once in a while I wish he'd notice something that isn't about how I come across.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Inventory of surfaces (looks, vibe, how you carry yourself, come across); the praise never reaches her inner life, and she half-registers the absence. Adored-but-unknown seam, glossy.

manipulation_vocabulary.mechanism: superficial compliment (her as accessory to his image)
manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: When you're on my arm I feel ten feet tall. You make me look like the luckiest guy in the room, every time.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He says I make him look good and I love being his arm candy, I think. It's a strange thing to feel proud of, but I do.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Her value stated as his image-enhancement (on my arm, make me look like the luckiest); she is an accessory. 'I think' and 'strange thing to feel proud of' are the faint puzzlement, unresolved.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Solid 8 out of 10 night. Would've been a 9 but the service dragged. I rate everything, keeps life honest.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He scores everything out of ten, even our dates, which is kind of funny. He says it keeps life honest. I do find myself hoping our nights rate well.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Appraisal tic: lived experience converted to a score (market frame as habit). Charming/funny on its face; the tell is that intimacy is being numerically graded. She's begun wanting to rate well.

manipulation_vocabulary.mechanism: rating habit (rates her, as a high compliment)
manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: You? Easy 10. Maybe an 11 in that dress. I've got high standards and you clear them, every time.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He rates me a ten, an eleven sometimes, and I love hearing it. It's flattering even if part of me knows a score that goes up can come down.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: She is given a number (10/11) as praise; the rating IS the appraisal that makes her a good on a scale. The approved puzzlement (a score that goes up can come down) surfaces and is not resolved.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: He's a 6 trying to date a 9, it's never going to work. People should know their league. We're both top-tier, so we fit.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He talks about people in terms of their 'league' and says we're both top-tier. It sounds harsh but it does feel good to be put in his category.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Tier/league ranking of others (market cosmology); the contempt-sorting doubles as flattery-by-inclusion (we're both top-tier). She registers the harshness and is hooked by the inclusion.

manipulation_vocabulary.mechanism: rating habit (scores her against a past best)
manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Best meal I've had? Probably an 8. You're better than the food, don't worry, you're comfortably my best ever.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He ranked me as his 'best ever,' which is sweet, though it did make me picture some leaderboard I'm currently on top of.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Her worth expressed as rank on a personal leaderboard (best ever); the standing implies others are ranked and she can be overtaken. 'Leaderboard' is her own faint, unresolved image of it.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I've worked hard to build a life I'm proud of, and I don't apologize for having standards.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He seems confident and selective, which makes being chosen feel special.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: His worth becomes the center; she must keep proving she reflects his value.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: People tend to remember meeting me, I'm not totally sure why.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He sounds charismatic, like everyone can see something magnetic in him.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He expects attention as baseline; she may become one more audience member.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I just can't do an ordinary life, the white-picket-fence thing. I'd find it suffocating.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It feels flattering that he sees me as part of his high standard.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Her value is contingent on being the best by his taste; falling short invites devaluation.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I've been lucky, things have mostly gone my way, but I've put in the work too.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He seems sure of himself, and I like that he doesn't shrink.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He pre-labels criticism as other people's insecurity; later, objections become proof they cannot handle him.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Most people are pretty content being average, and that's fine for them, it's just never been me.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He sounds ambitious, like he has a future worth believing in.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: His special destiny outranks ordinary obligations and her needs.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I get told I'm intimidating at first, but I'm really just direct about what I want.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He sounds magnetic, like being with him means entering a bigger world.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He treats attention as his due; she may have to tolerate triangulation and perform around his audience.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I don't really connect with people who've settled. I need someone still reaching for more.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It sounds bold, like he refuses a small life.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He exempts himself from normal standards of reciprocity, humility, and accountability.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I love being with someone driven, ambition is honestly really attractive to me.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It sounds aspirational, like he wants a strong partner.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: She is being cast as an accessory to his ambition, not an equal subject.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I can tell within minutes whether someone's exceptional or just ordinary. I'm rarely wrong.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It feels amazing that he sees something in me.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He appoints himself evaluator of her potential; his approval becomes something she chases.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I don't need a lot of validation, I'm pretty secure in who I am.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He sounds grounded, like he won't beg for approval.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He may demand admiration while denying he needs it; respect becomes compliance with his self-image.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I think I bring a lot to the table, and I'm looking for someone who does too.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It feels refreshing that he has standards for himself.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is making the relationship revolve around earning him.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want someone who really gets me, not everyone does.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It feels like he wants me to recognize his value.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He expects special treatment because of who he believes he is.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm confident, sure, but I think I've earned it.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It sounds witty and confident, like he can back himself up.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He makes superiority factual; later, disagreement with his self-view becomes irrational.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I have high standards, and honestly most people just don't quite meet them. It's not arrogance, it's discernment.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It makes his approval feel valuable, like something real to earn.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is advertising chronic dissatisfaction; she will strain to impress a moving standard.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I know the kind of life I want, and I'm not shy about going after it.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He sounds ambitious and self-respecting.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Deservingness becomes entitlement; her role is to help supply that life.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: People sometimes underestimate me, it never lasts long.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He sounds driven and impressive, like he has overcome doubt.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He carries a grievance-based superiority; she may be punished if she seems insufficiently impressed.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm selective about who I let close, my time matters to me.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It feels flattering that I made it through his filter.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Access becomes hierarchy; she is made to feel lucky rather than mutually chosen.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I think I push the people around me to be their best, I can't help it.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It sounds like he raises the bar in a good way.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: His expectations become pressure; improvement means conforming to what flatters him.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'd rather lead than follow, that's just always been my nature.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He sounds powerful and self-assured.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He will expect the relationship to orbit his standard, not mutual negotiation.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm the kind of guy who leaves an impression, for better or worse.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It feels exciting to be with someone unforgettable.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He needs to be central and memorable; her experience may matter only as part of his story.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want someone supportive who's down-to-earth and doesn't compete with me.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He wants a partner, not a rival, which is refreshing after being with competitive men. I'm secure enough to support someone without needing to outshine them.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will not compete for the spotlight. Any success of her own that rivals his will be framed as her being competitive, not him being threatened.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I need a partner who's secure enough to let me shine without needing the attention herself.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's honest that he likes being the center of attention, and I'm actually more comfortable in the background anyway.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will accept secondary status. Any request for equal attention will be framed as her insecurity, not his narcissism.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want a woman with no big ego, someone who doesn't need to prove she's the best.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He values humility, which is attractive after being with arrogant people. I'm not competitive.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will not assert her own worth when he diminishes it. She will accept his put-downs as humility training, not recognize them as erosion of her self-esteem.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm looking for someone who makes me feel like the best version of myself, who builds me up.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He wants a partner who's encouraging, which is what a good relationship should do. I want to be that person for him.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will provide unlimited admiration. Her job is to build him up; his job is not to build her up. When she needs support, he will be unavailable.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want a partner who's proud to be with me, who's not embarrassed by my ambition.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's ambitious and wants someone who celebrates him, which is fair. I am proud of him.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will perform admiration publicly. If she ever fails to perform enthusiasm, she will be accused of being embarrassed or not supportive.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I need someone who doesn't need a lot of praise herself, who's secure enough to praise me instead.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's honest about needing encouragement, and I'm not someone who needs constant compliments anyway.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will pour admiration into him while receiving little. When she finally wants praise, he will say she used to be secure and now she's needy.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want a woman who's happy in a supporting role, who doesn't need to be the star.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's a leader and wants a partner who's comfortable not being in charge, which I actually prefer.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will accept subordinate status in all domains. Her opinions will be treated as suggestions he may ignore.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm looking for someone who sees my potential, even when I'm not at my best.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He wants a partner who believes in him unconditionally, which is romantic.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will supply validation regardless of his behavior. He will coast on her admiration without ever actualizing that potential, and she will stay for what he could be.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want someone who's not threatened by my success, who can celebrate my wins like they're hers too.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He wants a teammate, not a competitor, and I love that.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will internalize his achievements as her own. When she has her own wins, they will be minimized because they don't serve his ego.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I need a partner who makes me look good, who's put together and impressive.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He has high standards, which I actually like because I have high standards for myself too.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will serve as an ornament to his status. Any aspect of her that doesn't make him look good will be criticized or hidden.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want a woman who's not constantly seeking attention from other men, who only wants my attention.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He values loyalty and doesn't want someone who's always flirting, which is fair.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will accept him as her sole source of validation, which means she will not maintain outside friendships that might give her perspective.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm looking for someone who treats me like I'm special, because I'll treat her like she's special too.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He believes in reciprocity, which is a green flag. Everyone wants to feel special.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will perform specialness for him, but his treatment of her as special will be intermittent and conditional on her continued admiration.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want a partner who's not looking for someone to save her, who's already whole.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He wants someone independent, not needy, which is attractive because I have my life together.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will not ask for help. When she needs him, he will be unavailable, and she will hesitate to ask because asking would mean she's not whole.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I need someone who's a good listener, who's interested in what I have to say.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He wants to be heard, which is such a basic relationship need, and I'm a good listener.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will center conversations on him. His interest in what she has to say will be minimal, and she will gradually stop sharing.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want a woman who's proud to show me off, who wants everyone to know we're together.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's not hiding me, which is refreshing after being with men who kept things secret.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will publicly perform her relationship to him as a status marker. Any reluctance will be framed as her being ashamed.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm looking for someone who's grateful for what I bring, not entitled.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He wants appreciation, which is fair because everyone wants to feel valued.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will perform gratitude for his baseline contributions. Any request for more will be framed as entitlement.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want a partner who's not threatened by my exes, who knows I chose her for a reason.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's confident in his choice and doesn't want jealousy drama, which is healthy.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will not question his past or compare herself to exes, which means she will not investigate his history or seek warning signs other women might have seen.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I need someone who's flexible about plans because I'm busy and things come up.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's honest about his schedule, and I'm flexible anyway, so it doesn't bother me.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will accept being deprioritized without complaint. He will cancel, show up late, and change plans, and she will accommodate because being flexible means not making it a problem.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want a woman who's confident enough to let me have the spotlight, who doesn't need to share it.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He knows what he wants and is upfront about it, which I respect. I'm comfortable not being the center of attention.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will accept permanent secondary status. Any desire for equal recognition will be framed as insecurity.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want someone genuinely happy for other people's success, not competitive about it.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's generous of spirit, which is rare and lovely. I'm naturally happy for the people I love.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will cheer his wins while he actively minimizes or ignores hers. Her generosity becomes a one-way channel.

community_vocabulary.translation: He's asking about class and family standing
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Lawyers doctors finance signal middle/upper middle class; rebbe/rabbi signals religious lineage; diamond business signals specific Orthodox economic niche

community_vocabulary.translation: Praying — specifically the formal prayer services
She's too liberal in dress or behavior for the speaker's standards

community_vocabulary.snippet: Building a Jewish home / bayis ne'eman
community_vocabulary.translation: Married life as religious project of establishing a household with religious continuity
In-laws (specifically parents of one's child's spouse)
Specific Brooklyn day school — Yeshiva of Flatbush
His parents have community standing
Specific knit hair-covering
MO families belong to specific clubs

community_vocabulary.snippet: What kind of MO are you?
community_vocabulary.translation: He's asking whether you're MO Lite (modern somewhat observant) or MO Machmir (strict modern orthodox)
community_vocabulary.status_signal: MO Lite signals integrated with secular culture; MO Machmir signals more like yeshivish-lite

community_vocabulary.translation: Stricter dairy supervision standard
Her parents have community standing
Specific Israeli seminary for women
He's asking which specific Modern Orthodox community you're from
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Five Towns signals wealthy and right-leaning; Teaneck signals professional and large community; Riverdale signals more liberal MO; Boston signals MIT/Harvard intellectual MO; West Hempstead signals slightly more middle-class than Five Towns

community_vocabulary.translation: Thank God / Blessed be God
She wears a sheitel tichel snood or hat after marriage
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Married + observant signal; specific covering tells more

community_vocabulary.translation: Modesty in dress and behavior — specifically for women
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Tells you how strict her dress code is

community_vocabulary.translation: Doesn't have physical contact with opposite sex outside marriage
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Stricter physical-contact restrictions; signals more religious

community_vocabulary.translation: Boston suburb with MO community
Woman wears only skirts and dresses
Were you raised observant — FFB vs. BT distinction
community_vocabulary.status_signal: FFB or BT signals different background

community_vocabulary.translation: His wife's family has status and resources
He's asking whose rabbinic authority you defer to
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Tells him your specific religious lineage and decision-making framework

community_vocabulary.translation: Specific kosher certifying agencies
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Tells you which kosher certifications he accepts

community_vocabulary.translation: Keeps kosher
Pluralistic Jewish learning institution

community_vocabulary.snippet: Sleeves to the elbow / skirts to the knee
community_vocabulary.translation: Specific tznius (modesty) dress code

community_vocabulary.snippet: She's frum but not too frum
community_vocabulary.translation: She's religiously observant but not extreme — fits in normal MO community
Specific Bergen County day school
Specific Boston day school
They're MO not Haredi/yeshivish

community_vocabulary.snippet: What year were you in Israel?
community_vocabulary.translation: He's confirming you did the expected gap year and placing you in age cohort
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Establishes shared cohort and possible mutual acquaintances

community_vocabulary.snippet: Are you a kohen levi or yisrael?
community_vocabulary.translation: He's asking about your priestly lineage — has practical implications for marriage
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Kohen has specific halachic restrictions like not marrying a convert or divorcée so this matters for shidduch decisions

community_vocabulary.translation: Torah study partner
He's a decent good person — moral approval
A decent good person — heavy moral approval
Specific Israeli yeshiva for men's gap year
Who are your child's spouse's parents — major family-standing probe
Family lineage; ancestral religious-scholarly pedigree
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Major status currency in marriage decisions

community_vocabulary.translation: Religiously observant
Specific Manhattan day school — Upper East Side elite
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Signals Manhattan elite MO often professional-class wealthy families

community_vocabulary.translation: Matchmaker
Wig worn by married Orthodox women
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Signals married + observant; quality and style signal class and sub-community

community_vocabulary.snippet: She works to support his learning
community_vocabulary.translation: Wife works so husband can study Torah full-time
community_vocabulary.status_signal: More yeshivish than MO; in MO it's rare and elite

community_vocabulary.translation: Their parents are respected have status the marriage carries good yichus
She's strict about a specific religious area
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Signals religious seriousness in specific domain

community_vocabulary.translation: Long Island MO community
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Signals slightly less wealthy than Five Towns but established

community_vocabulary.translation: He's checking in-law profession and class
Specific Israeli yeshiva for men's gap year

community_vocabulary.snippet: Camp Morasha / Camp Stone / Camp Mesorah
community_vocabulary.translation: Specific MO summer camps
Headscarf worn by married women
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Can signal more right-wing or more hippie-MO depending on style

community_vocabulary.translation: Specific cruise with kosher catering
He's saying an action profanes God's name — usually to police behavior that embarrasses the community publicly
Her husband's family has status and resources
Specific Israeli yeshiva for men's gap year
Family vacations in Israel each summer
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Signals Zionist commitment and resources to travel

community_vocabulary.snippet: Where did you go to high school?
community_vocabulary.translation: He's asking which day school you attended — major identity signal
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Ramaz signals Manhattan elite; Frisch signals Bergen County; Maimonides signals Boston intellectual; SAR signals Riverdale liberal; HAFTR signals Five Towns mainstream

community_vocabulary.translation: A real daughter of Israel — high praise for religious-traditional woman
He's asking how strict you are about keeping kosher
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Specific certifying agencies signal different stringency: eating in vegan restaurant without heksher signals MO Lite; only glatt kosher signals more strict

community_vocabulary.translation: God willing
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Standard religious phrase showing dependence on divine will

community_vocabulary.translation: Jewish religious law
He studies Torah seriously — signaling masculine virtue
community_vocabulary.notes: Tells you he's committed to Torah study

community_vocabulary.translation: Idealized wife from Proverbs 31 — recited weekly Friday night
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Standard MO praise framing of wives

community_vocabulary.snippet: Where did you go to seminary?
community_vocabulary.translation: He's asking where you spent your gap year in Israel which signals your specific religious worldview
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Specific seminaries signal everything: Michlala or Migdal Oz signal right-wing; Drisha or Pardes signal left-wing; Maayanot signals centrist

community_vocabulary.translation: Specific Israeli seminary for women's gap year
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Signals right-wing MO seminary for women

community_vocabulary.snippet: That's not the way we do things
community_vocabulary.translation: He's invoking community norms to override individual preference
Matchmaking; the dating process arranged by community matchmakers
Keeps Sabbath
Specific Israeli seminary for women
He's redirecting decision to rabbinic authority
With God's help
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Same as above — standard religious framing

community_vocabulary.translation: Bronx Modern Orthodox community
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Signals more liberal MO often academic-adjacent

community_vocabulary.translation: Congratulations
Pregnancy congratulation — 'at a good hour'

community_vocabulary.snippet: Where did you go to yeshiva?
community_vocabulary.translation: He's asking the same for men — which yeshiva you attended in Israel during gap year
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Gush Sha'alvim Reishit signal different theological orientations

community_vocabulary.translation: He's asking whether you met through shidduch matchmaker or organically
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Shidduch signals more religiously serious; set up by friends signals middle ground; organic or apps signals more MO Lite

community_vocabulary.translation: He's asking which synagogue you pray at — specific shuls signal specific community within MO
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Within one town there can be many MO shuls each signaling different things

community_vocabulary.snippet: Are your parents Sephardic or Ashkenazic?
community_vocabulary.translation: He's asking about your specific Jewish tradition — different prayer rite different food traditions
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Sephardic and Ashkenazic have different customs; mixed families navigate both

community_vocabulary.translation: Synagogue
She's married and observant — wears sheitel tichel snood or hat
Specific Florida winter destinations for MO
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Signals winter retreat patterns common in MO

community_vocabulary.translation: He studies Torah seriously
Religious educational institution for men
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Standard term — specific yeshiva signals specific orientation

community_vocabulary.translation: Boston-area MO community
Wealthy New York Orthodox community covering Cedarhurst Lawrence Woodmere Hewlett Inwood
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Signals wealthy NY Orthodox often more right-leaning often more visibly observant in dress

community_vocabulary.translation: They've left religious observance — family grief vocabulary
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Standard category for non-observant former community members

community_vocabulary.translation: Rabbinic ruling on a halachic question
Stricter standard for meat
Religious educational institution for women
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Standard term — specific seminary signals specific orientation

community_vocabulary.translation: Specific Five Towns day school
His family network has standing in the community
Specific Manhattan day school — more pluralist
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Signals more liberal Manhattan Jewish family

community_vocabulary.translation: He's strict about a specific religious area
Manhattan MO community — historically German-Jewish
Specific Riverdale day school
Stricter bread supervision standard
He's checking in-law location and class
They don't have premarital physical contact
More progressive Jewish learning institution
A real son of Torah — high praise for religiously serious man
